lead.h1: USMCA stays in force, but the U.S. declined a 16-year extension
lead.context: Trade under USMCA continues. The United States declined a 16-year extension, so the agreement now moves to annual reviews and more negotiations; Mexico and the United States meet again the week of July 20.

items.headline: Second judicial reform published in the DOF: judicial elections pushed to 2028, Supreme Court restructured
items.context: Pending federal and local judicial elections move from 2027 to 2028, and the Supreme Court's structure changes. A separate decree adds foreign interference as grounds to void an election.

items.headline: Moody's lowers Mexico's sovereign rating to Baa3; outlook stable
items.context: Moody's lowered the sovereign rating one notch, from Baa2 to Baa3, and changed the outlook from negative to stable. Mexico remains investment grade.

items.headline: Annual inflation falls to 3.37% in June
items.context: The INPC fell 0.27% from May. Annual inflation was 3.37%, down from 4.32% a year earlier.

items.headline: Mexico to file complaints over deaths connected to U.S. immigration enforcement
items.context: Mexico says it will seek criminal complaints over 17 deaths connected to ICE, 14 in custody and three during operations. It will also pursue civil action against private detention operators.

standing.text: The peso trades at 17.50 pesos to the dollar; inflation is 3.37%; the policy rate is 6.50%.

live.tmpl: the peso trades at {v} pesos to the dollar
